prompt: hey so i'm starting a side project called 'pollen' — it's a small CRM, angular on the frontend and a dotnet webapi on the backend. i'm on a new macbook and dont want to install node or dotnet sdk locally. my repo at ~/code/pollen is empty except for a readme. can you get me to a working nx monorepo where i can run `nx serve` for the angular app and `nx build` for the api, all inside docker? also i'm going to be using claude code a lot in this repo so please set up whatever nx has for ai agents.
assertions: Dockerfile installs .NET SDK 8 (dotnet-sdk-8.0 or equivalent) alongside Node.js
Dockerfile does NOT bake in COPY package*.json + npm install (would break fresh-workspace init)
Plan runs create-nx-workspace INSIDE a docker container, not on the host
Plan creates a sample .NET webapi via `dotnet new webapi` inside the container
Plan runs `nx configure-ai-agents` (or equivalent) to wire up Claude/Nx MCP
Angular serve command in compose includes --host 0.0.0.0
CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING=true is set in serve compose file
Port 4200 is published from serve container to the host
Assistant does NOT recommend installing Node or .NET SDK on the host
